commit.message:
feat(eks): install VPA and add memory autoscaling for APISIX (#4443)

* feat(eks): install VPA and add memory autoscaling for APISIX

Install the Vertical Pod Autoscaler (VPA) from the official Kubernetes
autoscaler Helm chart (kubernetes.github.io/autoscaler, chart 0.8.1,
VPA 1.6.0) as a core cluster capability across all EKS clusters.

VPA is configured with all three components enabled:
- recommender: analyses historical usage and generates recommendations
- updater: applies recommendations via in-place resize or eviction
- admissionController: mutates new pod requests at creation time
  (registerWebhook: true, failurePolicy: Ignore for fail-open behaviour)

The updater runs with --feature-gates=InPlaceOrRecreate=true to take
advantage of in-place pod resource updates on K8s 1.33+ (cluster is
currently 1.35), avoiding eviction when memory can be resized in place.

A VerticalPodAutoscaler object is added for the apache-apisix Deployment
targeting memory only (controlledResources: [memory]). CPU scaling
authority is left entirely to the existing HPA, which scales replicas
3→5 at 50% CPU utilisation. Splitting authority avoids the known HPA/VPA
conflict where VPA adjusting CPU requests distorts the HPA's utilisation
denominator.

A PodDisruptionBudget (maxUnavailable: 1) is enabled on the APISIX Helm
release to ensure at most one gateway pod is disrupted at a time if the
updater falls back to eviction.

The QA stack memory floor is raised to 1500Mi so the initial static
request (and VPA minAllowed) reflects observed actual usage. VPA will
continue to right-size from there based on real utilisation data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update src/ol_infrastructure/infrastructure/aws/eks/apisix_official.py

Co-authored-by: gemini-code-assist[bot] <176961590+gemini-code-assist[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(eks): use minAvailable=2 for APISIX PDB instead of maxUnavailable=1

Switching from maxUnavailable to minAvailable biases toward maintaining
a minimum serving pool at any replica count. With maxUnavailable=1 the
budget becomes unnecessarily strict as the HPA scales pods above 3;
minAvailable=2 keeps at least 2 pods available while allowing wider
disruption tolerance at higher replica counts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: publish metadata modal with required/optional tabs (#82)

## Summary

When a user clicks **Publish** and required metadata (title, slug) is
missing, a modal opens instead of publishing directly. This prevents
publishing documents without essential metadata.

## Modal Design

Two tabs following the app's consistent styling:

- **Required** — Title (text input) and Slug (auto-generated from title,
editable)
- **Optional** — Published at (datetime-local) and Tags (tag input with
Enter/comma to add)

All fields are pre-populated from the editor store. The user can review
and override values before confirming.

## Changes

### New
- **`PublishModal.vue`** — Tabbed modal using BaseModal, consistent with
existing modal styling (catppuccin colors, field classes, button styles)

### Modified
- **`App.vue`** — Publish flow now checks required fields; shows modal
if missing; saves title to Sanity before publishing when updated via
modal
- **`saveDocument.ts`** (API) — `blocks` field is now optional; at least
one of `blocks` or `title` must be provided. Enables title-only saves
from the publish modal
- **`api.ts`** (frontend) — `apiSaveDocument` signature updated: blocks
parameter is now optional
- **`vite.config.ts`** — Dev proxy updated to match new optional blocks
behavior

### Tests
- 4 new tests: title-only save (API + frontend), missing blocks+title
validation, body field omission
- All 123 tests pass (63 app + 60 API)

## Notes
- Tags and publishedAt are stored in the editor store only (not yet
persisted to Sanity). This is a pre-existing limitation, not introduced
by this PR.
- Slug is set at draft creation time. The modal allows editing the slug
in the store metadata.

commit.message:
headlamp: bypass forward-auth for OIDC endpoints

The authentik forward-auth middleware was intercepting /oidc and /oidc-callback
requests, starting its own auth flow before Headlamp's OIDC handler could run.
This created a 5-6 redirect chain that prevented the OIDC token exchange from
completing.

Split IngressRoute into two rules:
- /oidc and /oidc-callback: skip forward-auth (Headlamp handles OIDC natively)
- All other paths: keep forward-auth for access control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: sloppy-mode TailCall must Return, not Continue

When a TailCall in sloppy mode falls back to handle_call_any_receiver,
the result was DispatchAction::Continue — but TailCall is terminal with
no next bytecode instruction. This caused the interpreter to read past
the function's bytecode, producing wrong results for mutual tail-call
recursion (even/odd test).

Fix: convert Continue to Return so the callee's result propagates
correctly to the caller.

Fixes: test_tail_call_mutual_recursion_sloppy_small

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: revert and restart iteration on mid-phase resume

On resume, if state.phase is mid-iteration (not 'init' or 'done'):
- Log which phase was interrupted
- Revert working tree to baseline
- Remove partial iteration history entry
- Restart that iteration from the beginning

Prevents evaluator from seeing stale/error data from a crashed
implementor, and terminator from running without evaluator results.

commit.message:
fix(jit): revert relaxed Smi guards and remove arguments trampolines

Revert three speculative Smi-or-Boolean guards back to strict
movsxd+cmp Smi checks (LoadGlobal, LoadKeyedGeneric, LoadNamedGeneric).
The relaxed cmp-against-JIT_UNDEFINED guard allows booleans through,
causing incorrect speculation when booleans appear in arithmetic.

Remove CreateMappedArguments/CreateUnmappedArguments/CreateRestParameter
trampoline arms, restoring the unconditional deopt fallthrough. The
trampoline enabled sloppy-mode functions in Maglev but they produce
wrong results for mutual-recursive even/odd patterns.

fix(jit): revert unsafe Phi coalescing and optimistic speculation

Two Maglev JIT correctness fixes:

1. regalloc: Revert relaxed Phi coalescing and accumulator chain extension.
   The relaxed condition computed an effective_phi_end excluding uses in
   exit blocks, which is unsafe when those blocks are reached from inside
   the loop body. The chain extension overwrote live Phi values by walking
   backward through arithmetic ops. Restores the strict coalescing check
   (phi_end > back_start + 1).

2. codegen: Remove optimistic Phi speculation in compute_smi_guarded.
   The optimistic path marked loop-carried Phis as smi_guarded when only
   one input was Smi and all consumers were arithmetic. But the downstream
   i64 overflow guards (jo) do not trigger when adding two heap handles
   (~0x4_0000_0000), causing raw tagged pointers to leak into arithmetic
   results. Restores strict all-inputs-in-set check.

commit.message:
Improve telemetry robustness and fix Linux device ID (#2405)

## Describe your changes

### Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS
- Remove erroneous `@property` decorator on module-level
`get_telemetry_base_dir()` in `utils.py`. This turned the function into
a non-callable property descriptor, silently breaking device ID
retrieval on all Linux/macOS systems (Windows uses the registry and was
unaffected).
- Add graceful fallbacks when `HOME` is unset (common in containers):
tries `Path.home()`, then `/var/tmp`.
- Simplify `_store.py` with `mkdir(exist_ok=True)` to avoid race
conditions.

### Auto-disable telemetry in CI/CD environments
- Detect well-known CI env vars (`CI`, `TF_BUILD`, `GITHUB_ACTIONS`,
`JENKINS_URL`, `CODEBUILD_BUILD_ID`, `BUILDKITE`,
`SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI`) and auto-disable telemetry.
- CI check runs **before** heartbeat so no events are sent at all in
pipelines.
- User opt-out via `OLIVE_DISABLE_TELEMETRY=1` runs **after** heartbeat
(intentional: heartbeat still fires for user telemetry).

### Fix robustness issues
- **Cache entries silently lost**: Add missing `json.loads()` after
base64 decode in `_read_cache_entries()` — without this, cached events
were always dropped on retry.
- **Crash when logger is None**: Guard all `self._logger` call sites
with None checks.
- **File handle leak**: Close file handle if lock acquisition fails in
`_ExclusiveFileLock`.
- **Negative HTTP timeout**: Clamp deadline calculation to `max(0.1,
...)` in exporter.
- **Session leak on init failure**: Close HTTP session if exporter init
fails post-session creation.
- **Transport retry**: Catch `RequestException` (not just
`ConnectionError`) on retry.
- **Cache flush race**: Use `wait_for_callbacks()` instead of single
snapshot check.
- **Crash-proof public API**: Wrap all public `Telemetry` methods in
`try/except`.

### Other fixes
- Fix `docs/Privacy.md`: correct `OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_PATH` →
`OLIVE_TELEMETRY_CACHE_DIR`, document as directory path, add CI
auto-detection docs.
- Add `# noqa: S110` comments to satisfy CodeQL empty-except findings.
- Exporter validates `options is not None` with clear error message.

## Checklist before requesting a review
- [ ] Add unit tests for this change.
- [x] Make sure all tests can pass.
- [x] Update documents if necessary.
- [x] Lint and apply fixes to your code by running `lintrunner -a`
- [x] Is this a user-facing change? If yes, give a description of this
change to be included in the release notes.

Description of change for release notes:
> Fix telemetry device ID on Linux/macOS, auto-disable telemetry in
CI/CD environments, and fix multiple robustness issues in the telemetry
cache.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: auto-create draft document on first autosave (#81)

## Summary

When an authenticated user starts typing without an active document, the
first autosave now creates a new draft in Sanity automatically. This
removes the friction of manually creating a document before writing.

## Changes

- **`HomeView.vue`**: Updated autosave logic to auto-create a draft via
`apiCreateDraft` when no `activeDocument` exists
- Added `latestJson` pattern to prevent stale content saves during async
operations

blocks to ensure edits during draft creation or save are never dropped
- **Intro title detection**: Treats the intro placeholder title as
unset, falling back to 'Untitled'
- **Slug validation**: Falls back to `'untitled'` when `slugify()`
produces an empty string (e.g. emoji-only titles)
- **Empty title preservation**: Passes `undefined` (not empty string) as
title to save, preserving the draft's created title

commit.message:
fix: move @azure/functions to dependencies and add publish feedback (#80)

## Summary

Fixes three production errors traced via Application Insights:

### Root cause (issues 1 & 2): `@azure/functions` was a devDependency

App Insights trace:
```
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package '@azure/functions'
imported from /home/site/wwwroot/dist/functions/createDraft.js
```

`@azure/functions` was listed as a `devDependency` in
`app/api/package.json`. The SWA deploy action prunes devDependencies, so
no function handlers could register at runtime — all `/api/*` endpoints
returned 404.

**Fix:** Moved `@azure/functions` from `devDependencies` to
`dependencies`.

### Issue 3: Publish button had no feedback

The `publishDocument()` function logged errors to the console but showed
nothing to the user.

**Fix:**
- Added `publishStatus` state to the editor store (mirrors the existing
`saveStatus` pattern)
- Unified status display in the menu bar — publish status takes priority
over save status
- Publish button is now properly disabled:
  - While a save is in progress (prevents race condition)
  - While a publish is in progress (prevents double-trigger)
- When the current document is not a draft (was previously a silent
no-op)

### Testing
- All 119 tests pass (66 API + 53 frontend)
- Type check passes
- ESLint passes
- Pre-commit hooks pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

string operations that produce wrong results (HeapNumber instead of

background compilation active.

Add master bus limiter + enable auto-gain by default

Volume normalization:
- Add DynamicsCompressor as soft limiter on master bus (blepInput → limiter → masterChannel)

- Enable AutoGainController by default (was disabled) — auto-balances sample vs synth bus
- Apply autoGain setting when ToneEngine instance is first registered with audio store

* fix: replace inline static thread_local with function-local static in Model<>

Clang has a known bug where `inline static thread_local` members of class
templates produce a separate TLS variable per translation unit. This caused
`Model::the()` to throw "called outside of a simulation scope" on Clang even
when a `ModelScope` was active, because the scope wrote to one TU's copy of
`current_instance_` while `agent-meat.hpp` read from another TU's copy
(still nullptr).

The fix replaces the data member with a private static function holding a
function-local `thread_local` static. The C++ standard guarantees a single
instance per thread for function-local statics regardless of how many TUs
instantiate the template:

    static Model<TSeq> *& current_instance_() {
        thread_local Model<TSeq> * ptr = nullptr;
        return ptr;
    }

All three access sites are updated accordingly: Model::the(), Model::the_ptr(),
and ModelScope's constructor/destructor.

Also updates docs_src to clarify OpenMP usage (parallel runs only, not agent
loops) and adds a note on the infected-only sampling distinction in the
contact sampling documentation.

* Large system refactoring (addresses memory issues with ASan and simplifies the API). (#189)

## Major changes

### Refactoring

- Change the functionality of `Virus` and `Tool` functions: no longer capturing parameters via pointers (unsafe). Instead, switched to capturing parameter names via `std::shared_ptr` (validity checked in run-time).
- Replaced `GET_MODEL()` (`dynamic_cast`) macros with proper implementation of using `static_cast` for more efficient and easier to maintain models.
- The events are now part of `Model`. The event callback functions were passed via `std::function` to the `Event` class, and later dispatched by `Model`. There was no reason why to keep this design, so, instead, the update functions are called by enum (so instead of copying the function to the `Event` object, only an enum is passed), and then triggered as a private member of `Model`.
- The mixer functions used by the `Model` class are now member functions instead of member objects. Previously, these were stored as `MixerFun` objects, adding overhead and unnecessary dereferencing.
- The `Model::{array_double_tmp, array_virus_tmp}` are now proper arrays (so allocated in the stack) of 1024 x 2.

### Deleted/defunct code:

- Changed the functionality of the `Model::operator()` to not return a reference. This was unsafe behavior. Forcing users to call `Model::set_param()` instead.
- Removed unused (and redundant) constructor method for models. Left a single main constructor in place.
- The method `Agent::rm_agent_by_virus` is now `delete`d. It was not being used, and the method `Agent::rm_virus` already has the same functionality.
- The overhead of `ToolFunctions` that were shared between agents as a smart pointer has been eliminated. The functions are now stored as `ToolFun` objects in `Tool`. The same applies to `VirusFunctions`.

### Bugs

- The `Tool` and `Entity` classes lacked a `clone_ptr()` method. This was causing threads to share entities, which is most likely what triggered the ASan issues.
- One cryptic fix (proposed by Gemini, Codex, and Claude) was to free the memory of `Agent::tools` and `Agent::entities`. This change works with the code base and makes things safer (probably), so we kept it.

* Creates new Intervention PEP for the Measles models (#191)



## New intervention: PEP for Measles

Using the `GlobalEvent` as a base class, this PR creates a new feature that can be used with models involving post-exposure prophylaxis (PEP). In this case, we use composition and administer the PEP as either the Measles-Mumps-Rubella (MMR) vaccine or Immune Globulin (IG), depending on the agent's willingness to take it and timing. The model rationale is as follows:

1. The quarantine process runs. Agents who may be willing to use PEP are moved to quarantine for the moment.
2. The PEP intervention kicks in: Agents who (1) are willing to receive PEP and (2) are in either Quarantined Susceptible or Quarantined Exposed states are administered the PEP. These agents are also moved out of the quarantine process.
3. If exposition took place outside of the MMR window (usually 3 days), then IG is administered instead.
4. If the PEP is effective, exposed agents are moved to the recovered state.

The new feature can be applied as a simple `GlobalEvent` without having to modify the model.

In the case that all agents are willing to quarantine and take PEP, the transition matrix of the model should not feature any entry in the rows/columns of the Quarantine Exposed and Quarantined Susceptible states (this is included in the new test).

This PR includes two new tests: 14g and 14f for evaluating the Measles `InterventionMeaslesPEP` intervention on the `ModelMeaslesSchool`. The intervention can be added as follows:

```cpp
// Creating the PEP intervention and 
// setting it up so we can call it as a global event.
epimodels::InterventionMeaslesPEP<> pep(
  "Post-exposure prophylaxis for measles", // Name of the intervention
  1.0,       // "PEP MMR efficacy"
  1.0,       // "PEP IG efficacy"
  4.0 * 7.0, // "PEP IG half-life (mean)"
  7.0/2.0,   // "PEP IG half-life (sd)"
  1.0,       // "PEP willingness"
  3.0,       // "PEP MMR window"
  {MS::QUARANTINED_EXPOSED, MS::QUARANTINED_SUSCEPTIBLE},
  {MS::RECOVERED, MS::SUSCEPTIBLE}
);

pep.set_name("PEP intervention");

model.add_globalevent(pep);
```

`zero_points` buffers without bounds checking. This PR adds value-range
validation and tests for the `g_idx` input tensor in the `MatMulNBits`
operator.
